Dear Us. Donovan:
The undersigned lega] counsel for South Tahoe Pubfic
Utility 
District 
(DISTRICT), a tocal agency as defined under the
Polj,tical Reform Act, requests a formal- advice letter under Sec-
tion 83114 of the Government Code fron the Fair Political 
Prac-
tices Commission regarding Director Robert Mason, Director Louis
J. Pierini, and Director Steven J. onysko's participation in ac-
tion relating 
to se$/er and water capacity service charges,
rnitiqation fees, and other conditions for service by the DISTRICT
to the City of South Lake Tahoe Redevelopment Agency (AGENCY)
Project No. 1. 
Directors Robert l,lason, Louis J. Pierini, 
and
Steven J. onysko are el-ected Directors of South Tahoe Public
Utility 
District, 
a local agency, as defined in the Political
Reform Act. Director Robert Mason is aLso Chairman of the Board
of Directors of DISTRICT.
Director Robert Hason has served as an elected Director
of DISTRICT since 1984. Director Louis J. pierini and Director
Steven J. Onysko have served as elected Directors of DISTRICT
since November 24, 1989.
DISTRICT, CTTY. AND AGENCY
DISTRICT provides sewer service for collection, treat-
ment, and disposal of sewage to approximately l-7,000 connections.
The DISTRICT also provides water service for approximately 12,000
connections. The DISTRICT boundaries extend from Emerald Bay on
the north to the base of Luther pass on Highway 89 on the south,


and the state line between California and Nevada on the east, at
the south end of Lake Tahoe.
The City of South Lake Tahoe (CITY) is included within
the boundaries of the DISTRICT, but its boundaries are far l-ess
in size than the DISTRICT.
The CITY formed the AGENCY for redevelopment of a
defined area within a portion of CITY's boundaries, general,Iy
westerly of the above referred state line to the Ski Run
Boulevard area. 
The AGENCY cornprises only a part of the CITY
boundaries.
The CITY Councilpersons are also the Directors of the
AGENCY .
A map is attached showing the boundaries of the DIS-
TRICT, CfTY, and the AGENCY.
REDEVELOP},IENT PRO.IECT NO . ].
The
redevel- opnent
AGENCY. ThE
phases. The
NO. l- .
The
PROJECT NO. ]-
AGENCY has adopted a Redevel-opment Plan for
of the area of the CITY within the boundaries of
plan is proposed to be inplemented in two or more
first 
phase is referred to as REDEVELOPIi{ENT PROJECT
following projects are included in REDEVELoPMENT
Tahoe lJtarina Hotel ( 298 Hotel roons )
including Shops and Restaurants
Embassy Suites (400 Hotel suite units)
Some Affordable Housing
Linear Parkway
wetlands Project
Road\.ray and Drainage Improvements (L,oop Road)
REDEVELOP!,IENT PROJECT NO. l- wilL require l-,813 sewer
units equivalent to 568 plus single family residentiat unit sewer
requirernents, and water service. A sewer unit is a measurement
of estimated fl-ow and strength of sewage connected to DISTRICT
facilities.
older motels are being acquired and destroyed by
REDEVELOPI,IENT PROJECT NO. 1. 
The destroyed motels or motels to
be destroyed currently required l-,81-3 sewer units, the number
needed for REDEVELOP}TENT PROJECT NO. ]-.
Other projects or phases of redevelopment within its
boundaries are planned and contemplated by the AGENCY. Some of
these projects would require sewer and water service.


DISTRICT ACTION
The DISTRICT is required to furnish sewer and water
service to REDEVELOPMENT PROJECT NO. L if facilities 
and capacity
are available or can be made available. Facilities for se\"rer and
water, and capacity for water, may be required for service.
The DISTRICT is required by ]av, to charge the estirnated
cost of service. 
The DISTRICT sewer and r,rater enterprises are
required by law to be separate enterprises with separately ac-
countable funds and charges for each enterprise. 
charges from
one enterprise fund cannot be used for expenses of the other en-
terprise.
The District 
has a Transfer of Sewer Unit ordinance
providing for the transfer of sewer units fron a parcel- upon
which the development is destroyed to a new undeveloped or pre-
viously developed parcel requirinq sewer units for service.
There are certain conditions of transfer required under the or-
dinance which sorne of the REDEVELoPI.{ENT PRo,f ECT No . 1 may not be
able to meet. 
The Transfer ordinance provides that the Board of
Directors of the DISTRICT may give relief from these conditions
in its discretion.
The existing Transfer of Sewer Unit Ordinance rras
adopted in years past. 
The fee for transfer of the sewer units
may or may not equal the estinated cost of sewer service for the
sewer units transferred.
If the Transfer of Sewer Unit ordinance is not ap-
plicable to aII of the sewer service required for REDEVELoPI,IENT
PRoJECT NO. 1 and relief is not authorized by the DISTRICT Board
of Directors where it is required to conplete a transfer, a nes,
connection for sewer service is required for some REDEVELoPMENT
PRoJECT No. 1 Projects. The DISTRICT has established a capacity
or connection charge for new sewer connections under a separate
ordinance. This charge nay or may not be equal to the estinated
cost of sewer service for REDEVELOPUENT PROJECT NO. l" Projects.
The DISTRICT has no Transfer of Water Connection Or-
dinance. Afl- $rater connections for development are treated as
new connections. A capacity or connection charge has been estab-
Iished for each ne\., connection depending upon the size. 
This
capacity charge may or nay not equal the estimated cost of v/ater
service to REDEVELOPI,IENT PROJECT NO. 1 Projects, including the
cost of new facilities 
required for water service to these
projects.
The payment of the estimated costs by AGENCY for
REDEVELOPT{ENT PROJECT NO. 1 to DISTRICT for sewer and water serv-
ice can be established through a l,temorandun of Understanding
Agreement executed by the DISTRICT Board of Directors and the


Directors of the AGENCY. The pa)anent of the estinated costs by
AGENCY can also be established by granting of relief fron an ex-
isting DISTRICT Ordinance and anendhent of an existing DISTRICT
ordinance authorized by the DISTRICT Board of Directors. Either
procedure requires action by the Board of Directors of DISTRICT
to charge the estinated cost and provide sewer and water service
to REDEVELOPUENT PROJECT NO. 1.
The DISTRICT has five elected Directors. Section 15072
of the Public utilities 
code provides in part as follo!'rs:
rrNo Ordinance, Resolution, or ltotion sha1I be passed or
become effective \r'ithout the affirmative votes of at least a
najority of the Board. rr

fhe final decisj.on by Lake Tahoe Inn as to joinder

sections.question_synthetic: Can Directors Robert Mason, Louis J. Pierini, and Steven J. Onysko of the South Tahoe Public Utility District participate in District decisions regarding sewer and water service charges, mitigation fees, and conditions for service to the City of South Lake Tahoe Redevelopment Agency Project No. 1, given their potential financial interests?
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document contains both incoming request (dated March 8, 1990) and FPPC response letter (dated May 3, 1990). Classified as incoming_request based on primary content. Response letter provides factual information but not formal FPPC advice/conclusion. No clear QUESTION or CONCLUSION sections with standard formatting. Document is truncated at end.

embedding.qa_text: QUESTION: Can Directors Robert Mason, Louis J. Pierini, and Steven J. Onysko of the South Tahoe Public Utility District participate in District decisions regarding sewer and water service charges, mitigation fees, and conditions for service to the City of South Lake Tahoe Redevelopment Agency Project No. 1, given their potential financial interests?

Director Mason and his wife, Roberta, own the office building for Director Mason,s architectural business as joint tenants and the building is not rented to the architectural busi- ness. Director Mason,s business is a sole proprietorship. embedding.summary: This document contains an incoming request for formal FPPC advice regarding potential conflicts of interest for three elected directors of South Tahoe Public Utility District who may need to vote on service charges and conditions for a major redevelopment project. The letter details the directors' financial interests, including architectural services income and real property interests that may be affected by the District's decisions.
